title: fraxinus ornus subsp. rotundifolia
intro: Fraxinus ornus subsp. rotundifolia, also known as the Manna Ash, is a subspecies of the Fraxinus ornus tree, native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bark and leaves of the Manna Ash are used in herbal preparations, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Fraxinus ornus subsp. rotundifolia can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures.
